title: herniaria cinerea var. virescens
intro: Herniaria cinerea var. virescens, also known as the green common sneezeweed or grey rampion, is a plant species within the Herniariaceae family. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving sore throats, and has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne.
